---
title: "03_Analyses"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

```{r Load, include=FALSE}

library(tidyverse)
library(vegan) # for NMDS and diversity 
library(lme4) # for GLMs
library(performance) # for model assumptions 
library(DHARMa)

# polen data
pollen_samples <- read_csv('Data/Pollen_and_Surveys/Pollen_samples_data_cleaned.csv')
# nest-level data summary
beehav_sum <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Behaviour_nest_summary_cleaned.csv')
# nest-level data all social statuses
beehav_full <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Behaviour_nest_full_cleaned.csv')
# pollen_samples and behaviour data
pollen_beehav <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Pollen_and_behaviour_cleaned.csv')


```

```{r Sociality, include=FALSE}

table(beehav_sum$Num_females_same_day)
# ignore: 11 nests had 0 observed females partaking in nest care
# 36 had 1 female at a given time partaking in nest care
# 3 nests had 2 females
# 2 nests had 3 females
# 2+3 / 36+2+3 = 12% are social

# mean number of females
beehav_sum %>% filter(Social_status_con == 'social')
# (2 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 3)/ 5  = 2.4

# first social nest
beehav_full %>% filter(Social_status_con == 'social')
# 2024-11-14 

# were these nests just social, or both social and solitary?
social_nests <- beehav_full %>% filter(Nest == 'J100' | Nest == 'J18' | Nest == 'J87' | Nest == 'J91' | Nest == 'J92')

# J100: 
 # 2025-01-16 - solitary for 1 day
 # 2025-02-04 - social for 1 day

# J18: 
 # 2024-11-14 to 2024-11-25 - social for 12 days (solitary for one day in between)
 # 2024-12-02 to 2025-01-16 - solitary for 45 days

# J87
 # 2024-12-17 to 2025-01-06 - solitary for 20 days
 # 2025-01-07 - social for 1 day
 # 2025-01-15 to 2025-02-04 - solitary for 20 days

# J91
 # 2025-01-16 to 2025-01-22 - social for 7 days
 # not active in subsequent recordings

# J92
 # 2025-01-16 to 2025-01-28 - social for 13 days


```

```{r Pollen_Collection}


### 2i. Flower Constancy

# how many samples are above 95% of one pollen type
pollen_samples %>% 
  filter(Count_scaled >= 285) %>% 
  summarise(count = length(unique(Slide_code)))
# 46 out of 83 samples = 55%


### 2ii. Pollen composition comparison within and between individuals

# arrange by date 
pollen_samples <- pollen_samples %>% arrange(Date_pollen)

# take bees caught more than once
pollen_samples_xs <- pollen_samples %>% filter(Num_of_samples > 1)

# take pollen types that occur in more than one sample
pollen_samples_xs <- pollen_samples_xs %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Slide_code) > 6) %>%
  ungroup()

# make sure the sum of pollen counts in 200 at least
pollen_samples_xs <- pollen_samples_xs %>%
  group_by(Slide_code) %>%
  filter(sum(Count_scaled) > 250) %>%
  ungroup()

# pivot wider
pollen_samples_wide_xs <- pollen_samples_xs %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen, Family, Count_scaled)
pollen_samples_wide_xs <- pivot_wider(pollen_samples_wide_xs, names_from = 'Family', values_from = 'Count_scaled', id_cols = c(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen))

# replace NA with 0
pollen_samples_wide_xs[is.na(pollen_samples_wide)] <- 0

# extract descriptors for merging later
desc <- pollen_samples_wide_xs %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen)


## Create NMDS

nmds_pollen_comp <- metaMDS(pollen_samples_wide[4:ncol(pollen_samples_wide)], distance = 'bray', k = 2)

# inspect

print(nmds_pollen_comp) # stress is tiny
stressplot(nmds_pollen_comp) # should look like stairs

# plot(nmds_pollen_comp)
# scores(nmds_pollen_comp)

# extract NMDS scores for bees 
nmds_sample_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_pollen_comp)$sites) 
  
# missing columns I want
nmds_sample_scores$Slide_code <- pollen_samples_wide$Slide_code
nmds_sample_scores <- left_join(nmds_sample_scores, desc)
  
# plot all bees
nmds_sample_scores %>% 
  group_by(Bee_ID) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  ggplot() + 
    geom_point(aes(x=NMDS1, y=NMDS2, colour = Bee_ID), size = 2) + # add site scores
    geom_text(aes(x=NMDS1, y=NMDS2, label = Bee_ID)) + # add species scores
    theme_classic() + 
    theme(legend.position="none")








```

```{r Pollen, include=FALSE}

# pivot wider
pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen, Subtype, Count_scaled)
pollen_samples_wide <- pivot_wider(pollen_samples_wide, names_from = 'Subtype', values_from = 'Count_scaled', id_cols = c(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen))

# replace NA with 0
pollen_samples_wide[is.na(pollen_samples_wide)] <- 0


### 2iii. Pollen collection divserity 

# take just the samples and calculate diversity
just_pollen <- pollen_samples_wide %>% select(-Slide_code, -Bee_ID, -Date_pollen)
diversity <- just_pollen %>% diversity(index = "shannon")
# the zeros are flower constant bees

# make df and rename with capital
diversity <- as.data.frame(diversity)
diversity <- diversity %>% rename(Diversity = diversity)

# add the descriptive info back in
diversity$Slide_code <- pollen_samples_wide$Slide_code
diversity$Bee_ID <- pollen_samples_wide$Bee_ID
to_join <- pollen_samples %>% select(-...1, -Family, -Subtype, -Count, -Count_scaled, -Season, -Abundance_for50, -Abundance_for200)
to_join <- distinct(to_join)
pollen_diversity <- inner_join(to_join, diversity)

# create day of season, better than date for model
season_start <- as.Date("2024-11-07") # first day of collected pollen
pollen_diversity <- pollen_diversity %>%
  mutate(Day_of_season = as.integer(Date_pollen - season_start) + 1)
# move earlier
pollen_diversity <- pollen_diversity %>% relocate(Day_of_season, .after = 5)


## Option 1 - absolute diversity

## GLMM 
# 
# mod_div <- glmer(Diversity ~ Site + Day_of_season + (1 | Bee_ID) + (0 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID),
#       data = pollen_diversity)
# # having just (1 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID) gives the error: number of observations (=83) <= number of random effects (=98) for term (1 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID); the random-effects parameters and the residual variance (or scale parameter) are probably unidentifiable
# # so having (1 | Bee_ID) + (0 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID) which has intercept per bee and random slope for day or season per bee, but not estimation between intercept and slope
# summary(mod_div)
# check_model(mod_div) # weird
# plot(simulateResiduals(mod_div)) # bad, removing (0 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID) didnt help much
# hist(pollen_diversity$Diversity) # skewed 
# 

## Option 2 - cumulative diversity

## make cumulative diversity 



# take column names of taxa
taxa_cols <- pollen_samples_wide %>%
  select(Ranunculus:Unknown_7) %>%
  names()

# make day of season
pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples_wide %>%
  mutate(Day_of_season = as.integer(Date_pollen - season_start) + 1)
pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples_wide %>% relocate(Day_of_season, .after = 2)


# pull the cumultive counts of pollen and types within each bee
pollen_samples_cumu <- pollen_samples_wide %>%
  arrange(Day_of_season) %>%   
  group_by(Bee_ID) %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(taxa_cols), cumsum)) %>%
  ungroup() 

# make cumulative diversity 
pollen_samples_cumu$Diversity_cumu <- diversity(pollen_samples_cumu[, taxa_cols], index = "shannon")

## GLMM

# lmer(Diversity_cumu ~ Day_of_season + (1 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID), data = pollen_samples_cumu) 
# gives error from overestimating: Error: number of observations (=83) <= number of random effects (=98) for term (1 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID); the random-effects parameters and the residual variance (or scale parameter) are probably unidentifiable

# # removed slopes 
# x <- glmer(Diversity_cumu ~ Day_of_season + (1 | Bee_ID),
#      data = pollen_samples_cumu)
# check_model(x) # not great
# plot(simulateResiduals(x)) # bad
# summary(x)
# 
# # with slopes but no correlation between intercept and slope 
# y <- glmer(Diversity_cumu ~ Day_of_season + (1 | Bee_ID) + (0 + Day_of_season | Bee_ID), data = pollen_samples_cumu)
# check_model(y) # not great
# plot(simulateResiduals(y)) # not great
# summary(y)


pollen_diversity %>%
  filter(Num_of_samples == 2) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Day_of_season, y = Num_of_subtypes, colour = Bee_ID)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.3, alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_line()

pollen_diversity %>%
  filter(Num_of_samples > 2) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Day_of_season, y = Num_of_subtypes, colour = Bee_ID)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.3, alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_line()

pollen_diversity %>%
  filter(Num_of_samples == 1) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Day_of_season, y = Num_of_subtypes, colour = Bee_ID)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.3, alpha = 0.7) 



#### awful
# c <- glmer(Num_of_samples ~ Day_of_season + (1 | Bee_ID), data = pollen_diversity)
# 
# summary(c)
# plot(simulateResiduals(c))
# check_model(c)


```

```{r}




### 2iv. Floral abundance

# make day of season
pollen_samples <- pollen_samples %>% mutate(Day_of_season = as.integer(Date_pollen - season_start) + 1)

# remove NAs
pollen_samples_noNA <- pollen_samples %>% filter(!is.na(Abundance_for50))


## GLMM

z <- lmer(Count_scaled ~ Abundance_for50 + Subtype + scale(Day_of_season) + (1 | Bee_ID/Slide_code), data = pollen_samples_noNA)
# removed Site because it was perfectly predicted by Bee ID, so it was being dropped 
check_model(z) # not terrible
plot(simulateResiduals(z)) # not bad
summary(z) # large residual variance and 0 for random effects
hist(pollen_samples_noNA$Count_scaled)

# trying making it a proportion?
pollen_samples_noNA$Prop_count <- pollen_samples_noNA$Count_scaled / 300

a <- lmer(Prop_count ~ Abundance_for50 + Subtype + scale(Day_of_season) + (1 | Bee_ID/Slide_code), data = pollen_samples_noNA)
check_model(a) # identical
plot(simulateResiduals(a)) # identical
summary(a)

# try binomial
ab <- glmer(Prop_count ~ Abundance_for50 + Subtype + scale(Day_of_season) + (1 | Bee_ID/Slide_code), data = pollen_samples_noNA, family = binomial()) 
check_model(ab) # not great
plot(simulateResiduals(ab)) # AWFUL HAHAHA
summary(ab)



# plot
pollen_samples_noNA %>% 
  ggplot(aes(Count_scaled, Abundance_for50, colour = Subtype)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.6, height = 0.2, width = 0)


## Partial RDA 

## make 2 matrixes, one for abudnance and one for pollen collected






# remove unknown pollen types because we have no abundance data 



```

```{r Pollen_and_Sociality}

### 3i. FLower constancy and social status

# total counts 
pollen_beehav %>% 
  group_by(Social_status_at_time) %>% 
  summarise(Count = (length(unique(Slide_code))))
# 11 social and 34 solitary samples
# flower constant counts
pollen_beehav %>% 
  filter(Count_scaled >= 285) %>% 
  count(Social_status_at_time)
# 6 social and 20 solitary
# 55% social and 59% solitary sampels were flower constant (of bees that we have both pollen samples and social statuses for)


### 3ii. Pollen diversity and sociality 

# pivot wider

pollen_beehav_wide <- pollen_beehav %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen, Subtype, Count_scaled)
pollen_beehav_wide <- pivot_wider(pollen_beehav_wide, names_from = 'Subtype', values_from = 'Count_scaled', id_cols = c(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Date_pollen))

# replace NA with 0
pollen_beehav_wide[is.na(pollen_beehav_wide)] <- 0


# take just the samples and calculate diversity
just_pollen_beehav <- pollen_beehav_wide %>% select(-Slide_code, -Bee_ID, -Date_pollen)
diversity_beehav <- just_pollen_beehav %>% diversity(index = "shannon")
# the zeros are flower constant bees

# make df and rename with capital
diversity_beehav <- as.data.frame(diversity_beehav)
diversity_beehav <- diversity_beehav %>% rename(Diversity = diversity_beehav)

# add the descriptive info back in
diversity_beehav$Slide_code <- pollen_beehav_wide$Slide_code
diversity_beehav$Bee_ID <- pollen_beehav_wide$Bee_ID
to_join_beehav <- pollen_beehav %>% select(-...1, -Family, -Subtype, -Count, -Count_scaled)
to_join_beehav <- distinct(to_join_beehav)
pollen_diversity_beehav <- inner_join(to_join_beehav, diversity_beehav)

# create day of season, better than date for model
pollen_diversity_beehav <- pollen_diversity_beehav %>%
  mutate(Day_of_season = as.integer(Date_pollen - season_start) + 1)
# move earlier
pollen_diversity_beehav <- pollen_diversity_beehav %>% relocate(Day_of_season, .after = 5)

## GLMM

b <- lmer(Diversity ~ Social_status_at_time + (1 | Bee_ID), data = pollen_diversity_beehav)
check_model(b) # bad
plot(simulateResiduals(b)) # bad
summary(b) 



```
